anti_claim: This standard is a public-safe schema contract projection only; it does not authorize release, provider calls, private-data equivalence, or whole-system correctness.

anti_claim_contract.rule: fixture-backed proof must state what it does not prove

anti_claims: This public standard does not copy private source bodies or prove public runtime completeness.
This public standard does not authorize release, hosted-public readiness, publication, recipient work, provider calls, or private-data equivalence.

Active v2 standard status is a source JSON contract state only; it does not expand release, publication, provider dispatch, private-root equivalence, product-readiness, runtime acceptance, or whole-system correctness authority beyond the authority_ceiling.

omission_receipt.reason: public standard carries schema and id refs only

omission_receipt_contract.rule: compressed views must list omitted bodies and drilldown refs

receipt_expectations: receipt must cite validator_id, input fixture ids, output artifact refs, omissions, and anti_claim ids

required_reentry: land each unresolved used_by_organs target as accepted_current_authority or remove/rename the target in this standard source
refresh aggregate doctrine-lattice projections only through the builder owner after source admission or source cleanup lands
non_laundering_rules: active v2 standard status does not accept unresolved used_by_organs targets
relationships.used_by_organs is a routing declaration, not runtime invocation or organ admission proof
standard registry draft status is inventory metadata, not evidence that unresolved targets are accepted
generated coverage, health, and entry-card residual rows are projections, not source evidence
used_by_organ_boundary.claim_ceiling: standard relationship residual only, not usage, acceptance, runtime, release, publication, provider dispatch, private-root equivalence, or whole-system correctness proof

source_coupling_contract.rule: if generated or projected, record source input digest and stale-source behavior

standard_payload.anti_purpose: A claim that something should work.
failure_modes: untyped artifact admitted into rebuilt substrate
current public microcosm surface treated as sufficient without coverage check
private source material exposed instead of synthetic/schema-only fixture
projection claims source authority without coupling receipt
standard_payload.ontology: A record that a command, validator, or runtime behavior actually ran and what it proved.

contract_projection_basis.authority_boundary: computed standard-lattice contract only; existing authority_ceiling remains the operative claim ceiling.

validation_rules: validate required fields and enum values against this standard
reject missing source_refs unless the fixture declares synthetic origin
reject authority upgrades not backed by receipt_refs

author.purpose: create a valid Workingness record instance

refine_instance.purpose: improve an existing Workingness record without corrupting authority

refine_standard_and_propagate.purpose: change std_microcosm_workingness_record and propagate through governed artifacts plus generated lattice projections

source_authority_ref: microcosm-substrate/AXIOMS.md#1 (JSON is contract; Markdown is projection)

refinement_provenance.method: bulk_structured_v2_contract_envelope; validator_refs, receipt_refs, authority_ceiling, anti_claims, relationships, and public_private_boundary preserved; no runtime acceptance, release, or source-body authority added

doctrine_kind_envelope.note: This standard conforms to the universal doctrine-kind envelope; markdown, Mermaid, Atlas, coverage, health, and entry-card views are generated projections of this JSON source.

selective.rule: relationships.used_by_organs declares route pressure only; it does not accept missing organs or prove runtime use

lattice_edges.authority_boundary: declared contract topology only; generated relation coverage remains computed by build_doctrine_projection.py

field_semantics.id: stable workingness_record instance id when present in governed instances
field_semantics.authority_boundary: states the highest supported authority for the instance
field_semantics.source_refs: names source evidence without copying private source bodies
field_semantics.status: explicit lifecycle state; not release or completeness authority
field_semantics.relationships: typed source-declared edges or residual pressure, never inferred proof
field_semantics.omission_receipt: records omitted bodies and drilldown refs for compressed/generated views
field_semantics.anti_claims: states what the instance does not prove or authorize

instance_schema.note: Required keys are projected from this standard source required_fields list; schema validation remains validator-owned.

schema_check.rule: instance validates against required_fields and this standard contract

source_boundary.rule: public instances cite source_refs and preserve public_private_boundary; private/raw/provider bodies are not exported

link_resolution.rule: required lattice edge targets resolve to governed instance ids; selective unresolved targets remain typed residual pressure

projection_freshness.rule: generated markdown, Mermaid, Atlas, coverage, health, and entry-card views are regenerable from JSON source; stale projection is a trust downgrade, not source authority

atlas_card.surface: agent entry / doctrine lattice projection / atlas card
atlas_card.note: agent-routing projection; must link back to JSON source

coverage_health.note: computed health; never evidence for source claims

compression.rule: preserve authority boundary and anti-claim before any status/count summary
compression.purpose: keep standard-governed instance rows navigable without turning projection counts into readiness claims
